data.title: Greencastle Announces Closing of Acquisition of Common Shares of Green Shift Commodities Ltd.
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - January 29, 2026) - Greencastle Resources Ltd. ("Greencastle" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has closed its previously announced acquisition (the "Acquisition") of an aggregate of 4,000,000 common shares (the "Purchased Shares") in the capital of Green Shift Commodities Ltd. ("Green Shift") pursuant to a share purchase agreement dated January 19, 2026 with an arm's length third party (the "Vendor"). Prior to the acquisition of the Purchased Shares, the Company did not hold any securities of Green Shift.

As consideration for the Purchased Shares, the Company issued 3,600,000 common shares (the "Consideration Shares") from treasury at a deemed price of $0.05 per share for an aggregate deemed value of $180,000. No cash consideration was paid.

The Acquisition was completed for investment purposes and is consistent with the Company's strategy to pursue selective positions in prospective resource companies and projects. Green Shift is engaged in the resource sector, and the Company believes that the Acquisition provides attractive exposure to potential commodity-cycle upside and complements the Company's broader portfolio focus.

Closing of the Acquisition has occurred following receipt of all necessary corporate approvals and acceptance of the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV") for the issuance of the Consideration Shares.

The Consideration Shares were issued under applicable Canadian securities laws and are subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day from the date of issuance, expiring on May 30, 2026. The Consideration Shares are expected to be listed for trading on the TSXV upon expiry of the hold period, subject to TSXV acceptance and compliance with applicable listing requirements.

The Vendor is arm's length to the Company within the meaning of applicable securities laws. No finder's fees or commissions were paid in connection with the Acquisition. The Acquisition does not constitute a "related party transaction" under Multilateral Instrument 61-101 - Protection of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions.

For additional information, please visit www.greencastle.ltd or contact:

Anthony Roodenburg

Chief Executive Officer

Tel.: 416-367-4571 ext. 222.

Notice regarding Forward-Looking Information

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

This news release contains "forward-looking information" and "forward-looking statements" (collectively, "forward-looking statements") within the meaning of the applicable Canadian securities legislation. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements and are based on expectations, estimates and projections as at the date of this news release. Any statement that involves discussions with respect to predictions, expectations, beliefs, plans, projections, objectives, assumptions, future events or performance (often but not always using phrases such as "expects", or "does not expect", "is expected", "anticipates" or "does not anticipate", "plans", "budget", "scheduled", "forecasts", "estimates", "believes" or "intends" or variations of such words and phrases or stating that certain actions, events or results "may" or "could", "would", "might" or "will" be taken to occur or be achieved) are not statements of historical fact and may be forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements are subject to a variety of risks and uncertainties and other factors that could cause actual events or results to differ materially from those projected in the forward-looking information. The forward-looking information contained herein is given as of the date hereof and the Company assumes no responsibility to update or revise such information to reflect new events or circumstances, except as required by law.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/282103

data.title: Arthur J Gallagher's quarterly profit rises on fees, commission growth
data.description:
Arthur J. Gallagher posted 24.5% rise in fourth-quarter adjusted profit on Thursday, as the insurance broker benefited from stronger commissions and fees amid solid demand.

Insurance spending has proved resilient as businesses and individuals prioritize coverage against financial risks, natural disasters, and other losses, even as they rein in spending elsewhere.

Arthur J. Gallagher's commissions rose to $2.06 billion in the quarter from $1.50 billion in the prior year. Total fees climbed nearly 34.8% to $1.2 billion.

Insurance brokerages act as intermediaries between clients and insurers, guiding customers in selecting policies that best match their needs, instead of selling plans directly. Brokers generate revenue through commissions based on premiums, tying their performance closely to the insurance industry numbers.

Peer Brown and Brown on Monday reported higher quarterly adjusted profit, helped by rise in commissions and fees. But ‌its organic revenue fell 2.8%.

The company reported adjusted net profit of $620 million, or $2.38 per share, for the three months ended December 31, compared with $498 million, or $2.16 per share, in the same period a year earlier.

data.title: KLA Posts Higher 2Q Profit On Product and Services Sales Growth
data.description:
By Amira McKee

KLA posted a higher fiscal second quarter profit on gains in products and services revenue.

The semi-conductor equipment provider logged net income of $1.15 billion, or $8.68 per share, in the period ended Dec. 31, compared with $824.5 million, or $6.16 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were $8.85 per share. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for $8.79 per share.

Revenue rose 7.1% to $3.3 billion, beating the $3.25 billion forecast by Wall Street, according to FactSet. Services revenue increased 18%, while products revenue rose 4.2%.

KLA expects third-quarter revenue in the range of $3.2 billion to $3.5 billion and adjusted earnings in the range of $8.30 to $9.86 per share.

Analysts polled by FactSet anticipate third-quarter revenue of $3.29 billion and adjusted earnings of $8.97 per share.

"As we look forward to calendar year 2026, KLA is a key enabler of the AI ecosystem and continues to uniquely benefit from the AI infrastructure buildout across all major growth vectors, including foundry/logic, memory, advanced packaging, and services." CEO Rick Wallace said.

Write to Amira McKee at amira.mckee@wsj.com

data.title: OSI SYSTEMS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
OSI SYSTEMS INC, a leading provider of specialized electronic systems and components, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's financial and operational performance, showcasing significant growth in revenue and profitability, as well as strategic business developments.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $464.1 million, reflecting an increase from the previous period driven by higher product and service sales.

Gross Profit: $151.6 million, indicating a slight improvement in profitability due to cost management and increased sales volume.

Income from Operations: $58.8 million, showcasing operational efficiency and effective cost control measures.

Net Income: $38.7 million, demonstrating a stable financial performance with a slight increase from the prior period.

Diluted EPS: $2.22, reflecting consistent earnings per share performance, supported by strong revenue growth and cost management.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Security Division: Reported external customer revenue of $334.7 million for the three months ended December 31, 2025, and $588.9 million for the six months ended December 31, 2025.
  • Optoelectronics and Manufacturing Division: Generated $92.8 million and $182.5 million in external customer revenue for the three and six months ended December 31, 2025, respectively.
  • Healthcare Division: Recorded $36.5 million and $77.3 million in external customer revenue for the same periods.

Geographical Performance

The company operates globally with significant contributions from its foreign subsidiaries, particularly in the United Kingdom, India, Singapore, Canada, and Mexico. Approximately 32% of cash and cash equivalents were held by foreign subsidiaries as of December 31, 2025, indicating strong international operations.

Sales Units

The Optoelectronics and Manufacturing division supplies components and subsystems to external OEM customers, as well as to the Security and Healthcare divisions, indicating a robust internal and external supply chain.

New Product Launches

The Security division expanded its product offerings by acquiring a provider of critical military, space, and surveillance solutions, enhancing its product portfolio and customer base.

Future Outlook

The company expects to recognize revenue on approximately 47% of the remaining performance obligations over the next 12 months, with the remainder expected to be recognized thereafter. The company is also evaluating the financial impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, which could affect the timing and recognition of certain deductions, impacting the effective tax rate and deferred tax balances in future periods.

SEC Filing: OSI SYSTEMS INC [ OSIS ] - 10-Q - Jan. 29, 2026

data.title: CIMPRESS plc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CIMPRESS plc, a global leader in mass customization and web-to-print services, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, highlighting significant financial and operational achievements for the recent quarter. The report showcases the company's robust revenue growth, operational efficiencies, and strategic investments in new production capabilities.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $1,042.2 million, an increase of 11% compared to the prior year period, driven by external revenue growth across all reportable segments and currency benefits.
  • Operating Income: $88.1 million, an increase of $7.1 million, primarily due to revenue growth and operating expense efficiencies.
  • Net Income: $49.5 million, a decrease of $12.1 million, partly impacted by lower unrealized hedging gains and lower interest expenses.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $1.95, a decrease of $0.41, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Cimpress operates through five reportable segments: Vista, PrintBrothers, The Print Group, National Pen, and All Other Businesses. Each segment contributes to the company's overall revenue with distinct product offerings and market focuses.
  • Geographical Performance: The company reported strong performance across major markets, with significant contributions from North America and Europe. Vista, in particular, showed growth across all major markets, contributing to the overall revenue increase.
  • New Production Launches: Cimpress has invested in new manufacturing capabilities, particularly in North America, to support market demand. This includes the expansion of production facilities in Mexico, which is part of the company's strategy to enhance its operational capacity.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company has implemented updates to its inter-segment transaction methodology, allowing for more efficient internal production capabilities. This change aims to optimize cost structures and improve pricing and operational decisions.
  • Future Outlook: Cimpress remains focused on leveraging its mass customization platform to drive performance and competitive advantage. The company is also monitoring the U.S. tariff environment and remains confident in its ability to manage potential impacts through supply chain optimization and pricing strategies.

SEC Filing: CIMPRESS plc [ CMPR ] - 10-Q - Jan. 29, 2026

data.title: Demand at largest US electric grid hovers near winter record
data.description:
By Tim McLaughlin and Laila Kearney

The largest U.S. electric grid on Thursday operated near record levels for demand amid frigid temperatures as weather forecasters predicted another Arctic blast over the weekend.

The PJM Interconnection, which manages the flow of electricity for 67 million people in 13 Midwest and Mid-Atlantic states and Washington, D.C., reported electric demand at about 140 gigawatts in the morning hours. The grid's all-time winter record for demand is 143.7 GW, set in January 2025.

Earlier in the week, PJM said that record would be smashed on Friday, predicting 148 GW of demand. PJM now says Friday will not be a record-setting day, with a demand forecast of about 142 GW.

PJM and electric grids in New York and New England have had to navigate congested high-voltage lines throughout their territories. Temperatures hovering above 0 degrees Fahrenheit (-18 Celsius) this week have caused power line overloads from a surge in electricity demand.

Meanwhile, power plant outages in PJM's territory surged to more than 20 GW earlier this week as cold disrupted gas-fired turbines and boiler equipment at coal-fired generators.

PJM forecasts lower outages for Friday, however, at about 15 gigawatts, or about 8% of the grid's 180.8 GW of operational capacity.

On Thursday afternoon, PJM spot wholesale electricity prices were about $150 per MWh, far below brief peaks that soared above $3,000 per MWh earlier in the week. Prices calmed down as temperatures warmed slightly and gas supplies became less constricted for power generators.

“In the event of another significant storm with severe cold, it remains possible that prices could spike once again," said Matthew Palmer, head of Americas gas research at S&P Global Energy. "However, the closer we get to March and the end of the traditional winter heating season this is less likely.”

data.title: LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Lockheed Martin Corp, a leading aerospace and defense company, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing significant financial and operational achievements, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the highly competitive defense industry. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's performance, strategic direction, and risk management strategies.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales: $75,048 million, reflecting a 6% increase from 2024 primarily due to higher product sales in Missiles and Fire Control, Aeronautics, and Space segments.
  • Gross Profit: $7,619 million, an increase from $6,930 million in 2024, driven by higher sales volume and improved cost management.
  • Operating Profit: $7,731 million, up from $7,013 million in 2024, with significant contributions from the Missiles and Fire Control segment due to increased volume and favorable profit adjustments.
  • Net Earnings: $5,017 million, a decrease from $5,336 million in 2024, impacted by higher interest expenses and non-service FAS pension expenses.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $21.49, down from $22.31 in 2024, influenced by the factors affecting net earnings and a reduction in weighted average common shares outstanding due to share repurchases.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in four business segments: Aeronautics, Missiles and Fire Control (MFC), Rotary and Mission Systems (RMS), and Space. Aeronautics focuses on advanced military aircraft, MFC on missile defense systems, RMS on helicopters and combat systems, and Space on satellites and space transportation.
  • Geographical Performance: In 2025, 72% of sales were from the U.S. Government, with 63% from the Department of Defense. International customers accounted for 28% of sales, with significant interest in the F-35 program from international partners and FMS customers.
  • Sales Units: The Aeronautics segment delivered 1,293 production F-35 aircraft by the end of 2025, with a backlog of 368 aircraft. The company also secured a contract for 296 additional F-35 aircraft, indicating strong demand.
  • New Product Launches: The company continues to focus on technology modernization and life-cycle cost containment for the F-35 program, with ongoing development and sustainment contracts.
  • New Production Launches: The company is expanding production capacity to meet increased demand, particularly for the F-35 program, and is investing in new technologies to enhance competitiveness in military aircraft design and production.
  • Future Outlook: The company is rapidly transforming its business to meet increased demand due to recent regional conflicts. It is focusing on expanding production capacity and harnessing technologies like AI and advanced networking to enhance defense capabilities.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces supply chain challenges, including shortages and extended lead times for certain materials, which have resulted in delays and increased costs. These challenges are expected to continue into 2026.
  • Strategic Partnerships: The company is deepening its connection to the commercial industry through cooperative partnerships, joint ventures, and equity investments to enhance its portfolio and access new technologies.
  • Human Capital: As of December 31, 2025, the company employed approximately 123,000 people, with a focus on attracting and retaining top talent through competitive compensation, benefits, and development programs.
  • Environmental and Regulatory Compliance: The company is involved in environmental remediation at some facilities and is subject to various environmental protection laws. It also monitors developments in climate change-related regulation and has a sustainability program to reduce GHG emissions.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Partnerships and Acquisitions: The company is actively pursuing strategic partnerships and acquisitions to enhance its portfolio, as evidenced by the $360 million acquisition of Amentum’s Rapid Solutions business to integrate advanced space and airborne mission capabilities.
  • Capital Management: In 2025, the company repurchased 6.6 million shares of its common stock for $3.0 billion and issued senior unsecured notes, receiving net proceeds of $2.0 billion. The company also repaid $642 million of long-term notes. Cash and cash equivalents increased to $4.1 billion, and the company maintained a disciplined cash deployment strategy, investing in capital expenditures and selective business acquisitions. The total outstanding debt was $21.7 billion, with no borrowings under revolving credit facilities at year-end.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue managing its debt levels and pension obligations actively, with a focus on maintaining financial flexibility and minimizing borrowing costs. It aims to further invest in business and technology to enhance capabilities and explore new business opportunities. The company also anticipates potential impacts from changes in U.S. Government payment policies and macroeconomic conditions, which could affect cash flows and funding priorities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Operational Risks: The company operates in a highly competitive aerospace and defense industry, with significant reliance on U.S. Government contracts. Changes in government priorities, budget constraints, or procurement policies could adversely affect operations. The company faces risks related to potential reductions in defense spending, budget uncertainties, and government shutdowns, which could lead to reduced orders or program cancellations.
  • Program-Specific Risks: The F-35 program, a major revenue source, is subject to performance, schedule, and cost risks. The company is also exposed to extensive procurement laws and regulations, which, if violated, could result in fines, penalties, or contract terminations.
  • Supply Chain and Technological Risks: The company faces dependency on suppliers, with potential disruptions in the supply chain affecting performance. It is investing in digital transformation to enhance efficiency, but failure to implement these changes could impact competitiveness.
  • Geopolitical and Macroeconomic Risks: Geopolitical tensions, such as sanctions and trade restrictions, could disrupt business operations and supply chains. Macroeconomic conditions, including inflation and interest rate fluctuations, present additional risks to profitability and cash flow.
  • Management Strategies: Management is focused on addressing supply chain challenges and regulatory compliance to mitigate risks. The company is adapting to changes in U.S. Government procurement strategies, including longer-term contract structures and new acquisition authorities. Efforts are underway to improve supply chain resilience and manage costs effectively. The company is also investing in workforce development to ensure it has the necessary skills to meet future demands.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could impact financial results. Interest rate changes could affect borrowing costs and pension obligations. The company is monitoring these risks and implementing strategies to mitigate potential adverse effects on its financial performance.

SEC Filing: LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP [ LMT ] - 10-K - Jan. 29, 2026

data.title: Bertelsmann's BMG weighing possible acquisition of Concord, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
BMG Rights Management, part of the German media giant Bertelsmann , is considering a cash-and-stock offer for music company Concord, which could be valued as high as $7 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

data.title: US mulls tapping Robinhood for 'Trump accounts' for kids, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The U.S. government is considering selecting fintech firm Robinhood Markets to help oversee the "Trump accounts" it plans to open for millions of children, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Robinhood has begun internal preparations in case it is selected to become a trustee for the project, the report said, adding that some brokerages, including Fidelity Investments and Vanguard Group, have so far not been on the list of candidates considered for the initial rollout or solicited about it.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. Robinhood declined to comment on the report, while the U.S. Treasury Department did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment.

U.S. President Donald Trump rolled out on Wednesday his government-supported investment accounts for newborns in the United States, in which the Treasury says it will deposit $1,000 for all children born between 2025 and 2028, with some 25 million families estimated to be eligible.

Trump on Wednesday urged American businesses to contribute to employees' family accounts. With no additional contributions, the administration expects the accounts to be worth $5,800 by the time the newborn babies turn 18.

A selection is expected to be announced soon, and the Treasury Department has considered selecting as many as three firms to serve as the initial trustees, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: Mondelez Names CFO Zaramella COO, Initiates Search for New Finance Chief
data.description:
By Paul Ziobro

Mondelez International named Chief Financial Officer Luca Zaramella its chief operating officer and has started a search for a successor for its top finance job.

Zaramella, who has been CFO of the food company since 2018, will hold both positions starting on Feb. 1. In addition to CFO duties, he'll now oversee the company's commercial operations in its four geographical regions as well as corporate sales, marketing and supply chain functions.

The maker of Oreo cookies and Ritz crackers said Thursday it expects to name a new CFO at a later date.

Zaramella joined the company in 1996 in Italy before holding positions of increasing responsibility across the world. Before rising to CFO, he served as corporate controller, treasurer and global head of financial planning and analysis.

Write to Paul Ziobro at paul.ziobro@wsj.com

data.title: Sandisk Beats Earnings. The Stock Is Soaring. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Tae KimAdam Levine

Sandisk reported better-than-expected earnings results. Its stock was sharply higher in after-hours trading.

For the December quarter, the company reported adjusted earnings per share of $6.20, compared to Wall Street's consensus estimate of $3.62, according to FactSet. Revenue came in at $3.03 billion, which was ahead of analysts' expectations for $2.69 billion.

Sandisk provided a revenue range for the current quarter with a midpoint of $4.6 billion, versus the $2.93 billion average analyst estimate.

"This quarter's performance underscores our agility in capitalizing on better product mix, accelerating enterprise SSD deployments, and strengthening market demand dynamics," Sandisk CEO David Goeckeler said in the earnings release. "The critical role that our products play in powering AI and the world's technology is being recognized."

Sandisk shares rose as much as 14% following the release.

Last year, hard-drive maker Western Digital spun off its chip-based storage unit, Sandisk, to unlock the value of flash memory data storage, which has become increasingly important in data centers and consumer devices.

This rapid growth is being driven by the artificial-intelligence data-center boom, which is creating shortages in memory and storage capacity. AI computing requires massive amounts of data at every stage of the process, and different types of storage are used for different tasks. Sandisk's storage chips, known as NAND flash memory, are faster — but more expensive — than hard drives, making them essential for performance-critical workloads where eliminating bottlenecks is paramount.

That bottleneck took center stage earlier this month at CES, when Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced that the company's next-generation AI servers will incorporate a massive increase in NAND storage capacity. Those servers are expected to begin shipping in the second half of 2026, and by 2027 could consume as much as 10% of global NAND supply.

Later, at a CES Q&A, Huang said that eventually NAND for this one AI function "will likely be the largest storage market in the world."

As a pure-play NAND company, Sandisk stock has climbed 76% since Nvidia's announcement.

At a technology conference in December, Goeckeler said that data centers will overtake smartphones as the largest source of NAND demand in 2026. Supply is already tight for the high-end NAND used in AI servers, and conditions are expected to worsen in the second half of this year. New manufacturing capacity from Sandisk and competitors such as Micron is not expected to come online until 2027 or 2028.

As a result, the focus of Sandisk's earnings report will extend beyond the remainder of its fiscal year, which ends in June, to management's outlook for next year and beyond.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: GE Vernova Gains After Earnings. Guidance Boost Was Enough to Lift the Stock. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

GE Vernova reported solid fourth-quarter numbers, but the real show was guidance. It went up — and just after the company had given 2026 and 2028 outlooks in early December.

Shares jumped in early trading on Thursday. Then the gains faded, leaving the stock bobbing around all day, before investors decided the earnings report was good enough.

The power generation technology company on Wednesday reported earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, or Ebitda, of $1.2 billion from sales of $11 billion. Wall Street was looking for $1.3 billion and $10.6 billion, respectively. A year ago, GE Vernova reported Ebitda of $1.1 billion from sales of $10.6 billion.

Ebitda was a little light, but orders at $22.2 billion far exceeded sales. And more orders led to higher guidance. GE Vernova increased 2026 sales guidance to a midpoint of $44.5 billion, up from $41.5 billion, and 2028 sales guidance to about $56 billion, up from $52 billion.

The new orders were also at better profit margins. For 2026, management expects an Ebitda profit margin of about 12%, up from 8.4% in 2025. For 2028, margins should be about 20%, implying Ebitda of about $11.2 billion. Wall Street currently projects Ebitda closer to $11 billion. That estimate was $7.4 billion a year ago.

GE Vernova shares jumped about 6% initially on Thursday, but gains faded, leaving them down more than 1% at one point in premarket trading. Shares were in the red early in regular trading, before they closed up 2.7% at $711.59. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average finished flat.

It is a tepid response to a bullish outlook. Investors might have wanted more 2025 Ebitda. Guidance implies Ebitda of about $5.34 billion. Wall Street projects $5.47 billion.

Starting points matter, too. GE Vernova has been a strong performer. Coming into Wednesday trading, shares were up 95% over the past 12 months, leaving them trading for 51 times earnings expected over the coming 12 months.

Still, the quarter looks solid. BofA Securities analyst Andrew Obin projected Ebitda of $1.5 billion. He didn't get that, but he also projected orders of $17.6 billion, well below the $22.2 billion received. Data centers should represent about one-third of fourth-quarter orders for GE's power turbines, with utilities also moving more toward natural-gas turbines to meet rising demand for electricity, Obin wrote in a preview report.

Obin rates shares Buy and has a $804 price target. Overall, 69% of analysts covering the stock rate shares Buy, according to FactSet. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%. Wall Street's average price target for GE Vernova stock is about $768 a share, up $76 since the company's December event, where it provided the prior 2028 outlook.

Along with earnings, GE Vernova provided an update on M&A. GE Vernova agreed to buy Prolec in October, and that deal will close in the coming days, contrary to prior expectations of a mid-2026 closing. Faster closing drove some of the revenue upside for 2026.

That said, a lot is going right at GE Vernova. CEO Scott Strazik is worried most about business execution amid all the industry tailwinds. Vernova must remain "hungry and humble" to capitalize on them.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Australia's Origin Energy lifts low end of 2026 APLNG production outlook, Q2 APLNG revenue rises sequentially
data.description:
Australia's Origin Energy on Friday raised the lower end of its 2026 production forecast for its Australia Pacific LNG, and posted a 10% sequential rise in revenue from its stake in that same project in the second quarter, helped by higher LNG sales.

The power producer now expects its 2026 production from APLNG — a joint venture with ConocoPhillips and Sinopec — to be between 645 petajoules (PJ) and 680 PJ, compared to its previous guidance of 635 PJ to 680 PJ.

However, it flagged a lower production contribution in the second half of the year due to fewer days and continued natural decline in the eastern and non-operated fields.

The company said revenue from its stake in APLNG was A$528 million ($372.13 million) for the three months ended December 31, compared with A$482 million in the September quarter.

Continued focus on operational performance at APLNG has helped in delivering steady production and revenue, and supported reliable gas supply for export and domestic customers, said Origin CEO Frank Calabria.

However, Origin realised $9.55 per million British thermal units (mmBtu) in the quarter for LNG sold from APLNG in Queensland, down from $10.08 per mmBtu sequentially.

Liquefied natural gas prices were under pressure during the last quarter of 2025 due to ample supply and muted demand in Asia, with benchmarks still coming off a sharp decline in 2025.

($1 = 1.4188 Australian dollars)

data.title: LMT: Plan amended to allow lump sum payouts for directors facing government conflict of interest
data.description:
The Directors Deferred Compensation Plan was amended to allow accelerated lump sum distributions if a director's new government role creates a conflict of interest, with compliance to tax and securities laws. Directors can defer fees into various investment options, and the plan remains a contractual, unsecured obligation.

Original document: Lockheed Martin Corporation [LMT] SEC 10-K Annual Report — Jan. 29 2026
